chronology.title: Generation 1 Active Window (1653-1662)
chronology.description: First matriarch active window in high-density network conditions.

chronology.title: Pandemic Fracture Window (1661-1662)
chronology.description: Population thinning event that introduces the first visible structural fracture.

chronology.title: Generation 2 Active Window (1662-1687)
chronology.description: Second matriarch transition arc through thinning but functional network conditions.

coreTruthSet.description: Generation 2 lives through network thinning and must actively maintain ties that prior density once maintained automatically.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: She grows up in a thinning but still functional network ... She must actively maintain connections.

coreTruthSet.description: The lineage is explicitly designed as a three-generation matriarchal line aligned to a 1653-1687 census world model.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Map your three-generation matriarchal line inside the 1653 -> 1687 census world.

coreTruthSet.description: Generation 1 operates inside a dense, high-redundancy network where system stability precedes visible fracture.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: She is born into a fully functioning, high-density network.

coreTruthSet.description: Marriage and alliance entry are framed as relational placement through matrilineal network logic rather than primarily romantic selection.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: She is not chosen romantically first ... She is placed relationally.

coreTruthSet.description: The 1661-1662 pandemic thinning is treated as the first visible system fracture and a trigger for lineage transition.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Death (1661-1662 Pandemic Event) ... the first visible fracture in the system.

settingAnchors.name: Census-Aligned Red River Lineage Grid
settingAnchors.description: Narrative operating map tying lineage decisions to nearby populations, settlement density, and relationship exposure realities.

continuityConstraints.title: Density to Thinning Transition Law
continuityConstraints.text: Book 1 lineage continuity must show structural shift from high-density redundancy to thinning-node dependence without abrupt world-model collapse.

continuityConstraints.title: Observer Middle-Child Bridge Emergence
continuityConstraints.text: Leadership credibility emerges through observation-first development and distributed kin training, not direct primogeniture preparation.

continuityConstraints.title: Relationship Value Under Thinning Pressure
continuityConstraints.text: As nodes thin, each tie carries higher systemic weight; conflict and trade scenes should reflect increased relational stakes per connection.

openResearchGaps.title: Which source chain supports each population figure used in the 1653-1687 census-aligned model?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to separate anchored historical estimates from interpretive narrative scaffolding.

openResearchGaps.title: Which concrete scenes best show observer-to-bridge development across childhood, post-loss kin training, and early mediation?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to convert lineage architecture into emotionally legible cinematic progression.

openResearchGaps.title: How did pandemic thinning impacts vary across Natchitoches, Yatasi, and Doustioni local clusters?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed for non-uniform loss portrayal and realistic transition dynamics.
